​ What is anyformat?
anyformat is a document processing platform that lets you:
 Upload documents (PDFs, images, scans)
 Extract structured data using AI
 Validate and refine results
 Output clean data you can actually use
You don’t need to be technical. You don’t need to write code. You do need documents and a problem to solve. 
​ What can I do with anyformat?
With anyformat, you can:
 Extract data from invoices, receipts, IDs, contracts, and more
 Handle unstructured or messy documents
 Standardize data across different formats
 Build repeatable workflows for document processing
 Validate and improve quality over time
 Export structured data to your systems
All without managing OCR engines, brittle rules, or manual review processes. 
​ How anyformat works
At a simple level, anyformat works like this:
1
Upload a file
anyformat reads the document and understands its structure.
2
Define what you want
You describe the data you need using fields and instructions.
3
Refine & validate
Review results, fix mistakes, and improve accuracy.
4
Run at scale
Apply the same logic to thousands of documents.
5
Use the output
Export clean, structured data in formats your tools understand.
You can keep things simple, or go deeper as your needs grow. 
​ Key concepts you’ll learn here
Before diving in, it helps to understand a few core ideas:
 Files - The documents you upload
 Schemas - The structure of the data you want
 Fields - Individual pieces of information (date, amount, name…)
 Workflows - Repeatable processes to extract and validate data
 Outputs - The structured results you use elsewhere
Don’t worry, we’ll explain each one step by step. 
​ How to use this documentation
This documentation is designed as a learning path:
 New to anyformat? Start with Core Concepts.
 Already using the product? Jump directly to Workflows.
 Looking for power features? Check out the advanced sections.
You don’t need to read everything, just what helps you move forward. 
​ Explore more

Outputs
Learn about structured results and export formats
Data and Structure
Define schemas and fields for your workflows

Anyformat - overview
Established
2024
Location
Madrid, -, Spain
Primary Industry
Software
About
Established in 2024 and based in Madrid, Spain, Anyformat focuses on document processing technologies. Alejandro Fernandez Rodriguez (COO), Juan Huguet Garcia (CEO), and Diego Perez Sastre (CTO) are the co-founders of the company. In November 2025, Anyformat raised EUR 3. 3 million in seed funding led by new investor Kibo Ventures, with participation from another new investor Decelera Ventures.
Returning investors 4Founders Capital and Abac Capital also participated in the round.
Anyformat specializes in advanced data extraction and structuring solutions, enabling organizations to derive insights from various document formats, including emails, PDFs, scans, reports, and audio recordings. The company’s core offering utilizes proprietary in-house models optimized for specific use cases, allowing for automated extraction of complex and heterogeneous documents without the risk of hallucination. This technology is particularly beneficial for industries requiring the processing of lengthy documents, such as financial services, healthcare, and insurance.
Anyformat serves a diverse client base, including leading companies across multiple sectors, who leverage the service to streamline processes such as data reconciliation, regulatory compliance, and customer relationship management. The products are deployed in private cloud or on-premises environments, ensuring data security and privacy while being utilized in global markets.
The revenue model of Anyformat is centered around B2B transactions, where enterprises engage the company’s services through subscription-based agreements or tailored partnerships.
The company plans to utilize the November 2025 funding to grow its engineering and product teams, which will help advance its roadmap for enhanced extraction features and the creation of unique error detection models.
Established in 2024 and based in Madrid, Spain, Anyformat focuses on document processing technologies. In November 2025, Anyformat raised EUR 3. 3 million in seed funding led by new investor Kibo Ventures, with participation from another new investor Decelera Ventures.

Anyformat specializes in advanced data extraction and structuring solutions, enabling organizations to derive insights from various document formats, including emails, PDFs, scans, reports, and audio recordings. The company’s core offering utilizes proprietary in-house models optimized for specific use cases, allowing for automated extraction of complex and heterogeneous documents without the risk of hallucination. This technology is particularly beneficial for industries requiring the processing of lengthy documents, such as financial services, healthcare, and insurance.
Anyformat serves a diverse client base, including leading companies across multiple sectors, who leverage the service to streamline processes such as data reconciliation, regulatory compliance, and customer relationship management. The products are deployed in private cloud or on-premises environments, ensuring data security and privacy while being utilized in global markets.
The revenue model of Anyformat is centered around B2B transactions, where enterprises engage the company’s services through subscription-based agreements or tailored partnerships.
The company plans to utilize the November 2025 funding to grow its engineering and product teams, which will help advance its roadmap for enhanced extraction features and the creation of unique error detection models.

HomeFundingWith €4 million in funding, Kabilio scales AI tools helping Spanish accounting...
FundingSpain-Startups
With €4 million in funding, Kabilio scales AI tools helping Spanish accounting firms lift productivity by up to 50%
By David Cendon Garcia
Share
FacebookTwitterWhatsAppLinkedin
EU-Startups has learned that Kabilio, a Barcelona-based company specialising in the application of AI to automate accounting and tax processes, has closed a €4 million pre-Seed funding round.
The round was led by international venture capital funds Visionaries Club and Picus Capital. The round also includes €200k in public funding from ENISA, reportedly making it one of the largest pre-Seed rounds in Spain.
“We have seen firsthand the challenge of attracting and retaining talent in accounting firms because the work is concentrated on repetitive tasks and peak workloads. We have also perceived a certain fear of adopting AI.
“It is precisely to change this that we created ​Kabilio, and the closing of this funding round gives us the definitive push to help these firms alleviate their teams’ workload and allow them to focus on providing more value to their clients, which is what they are truly trained for,” states Jose Ojeda, co-CEO and founder of Kabilio.
The funding round for Kabilio fits into a broader European movement of 2025 investments in AI-driven financial software.
Comparable activity includes Bluebook (Sweden), which raised €2.4 million pre-Seed to advance its self-driving accounting platform; Stacks (Netherlands), which secured €9.5 million to streamline month-end financial close processes using AI; and Plino (Italy), which obtained €650k to enhance SMEs’ financial planning and analysis.
Within this context, Kabilio’s €4 million pre-Seed is comparatively substantial, signalling investor confidence in AI solutions addressing accounting inefficiencies.
While other notable rounds have occurred in Northern and Central Europe, Kabilio’s success highlights Spain’s emerging role in this segment, marking one of the country’s largest early-stage financings in AI-powered accounting.
Robert Jäckle, Partner at Visionaries Club, highlights: “Kabilio brings AI to the heart of advisory firms not to replace accountants, but to make their work smarter and more valuable. They are not creating ‘hype’; they are solving a real operational problem. We believe Kabilio can become the leading platform for modern accounting in Europe.”
Founded in 2024 by Jose Ojeda (formerly of McKinsey, Rocket Internet, and 011h) and Álex  
Valls (formerly of Social Point, Exoticca, and 011h), the startup has developed a platform  
that uses generative AI to help accounting firms and their clients modernise the way  
they work together.
Kabilio was born from the conviction that AI will transform the advisory sector and from a commitment to support them in this transition. This mission has already earned the trust of nearly 100 accounting firms using its platform, who are seeing a productivity increase of up to 50%.
Florian Reichert, Managing Director at Picus Capital, adds that the platform “will enable advisors to collaborate more efficiently with their clients, leveraging AI to automate the most tedious tasks and generate strategic value”.
In Spain, there are approximately 65,000 accounting and tax advisory firms that work with millions of SMEs and freelancers. The current workflow presents significant inefficiencies: clients must manually collect and send information, which, due to their operational load, often happens late or incompletely.
This is exacerbated during quarterly peak periods, when advisors must concentrate a great deal of effort on checks and manual data entry within a few days.
Kabilio currently features three integrated products:
 Intelligent invoice processing: Allows receiving documents from multiple channels (email, Dropbox, etc.) and processing even the most complex invoices, such as intra-community and extra-community, reverse charge VAT, or multiple invoices in a single file. Furthermore, it suggests appropriate accounting accounts based on the content of each invoice and the company’s activity, with 97% accuracy.
 Advanced bank reconciliation: Securely connects with 99% of Spanish banks, reducing the need to request statements. The platform suggests matches between invoices and transactions using AI rules, allowing the advisor to view and edit all information in a single environment.
 Verifactu-Compliant invoicing: A modern, intuitive, and cost-effective product for SMEs and freelancers that allows for issuing customised invoices and synchronises​ all information with the advisor in real-time, eliminating the manual sending of data.
Additionally, the company is piloting its AI agent, Kabi, with several clients, which is being officially presented at Accountex Spain 2025. In its initial phase, it will allow users to query and extract information from the platform using natural language, and future versions will enable broader actions.
“We are experiencing a technological turning point similar to the arrival of software in companies two decades ago. Artificial intelligence will not only optimise processes but will also change the nature of work and the relationship between advisory firms and their clients. Our goal is to build the infrastructure that makes this new paradigm possible”, said Álex Valls, co-CEO and co-founder of Kabilio.

Logo
Barcelona-based Alinia raises €2.2 million pre-seed to enable safe deployment of genAI
Stefano De Marzo

Alinia AI, an alignment platform enabling companies’ safe and controlled deployment of generative AI, guided by their policies and business preferences, announces a successful €2.2 million pre-seed funding round. Speedinvest and Precursor led the round with participation from KFund and notable angels including Clem Délangue & Thom Wolf (Hugging Face Co-Founders), Tom Preston (GitHub Co-Founder), Xavier Amatriain (Google Core AI VP), and Oriol Vinyals (Google DeepMind VP of Research).
Significant adoption of generative AI is underway with spending set to hit $143 billion by 2027, but its rapid adoption comes with significant challenges. The problem? Managing control and safety in the face of risks to prevent inappropriate or brand-damaging content. Additionally, navigating upcoming regulatory efforts like the EU AI Act, Biden´s Executive Order, or Canada´s Bill C-27, which demand rigorous safety checks and clear documentation, also adds hurdles to mass enterprise adoption. 
Evaluating the performance and reliability of generative AI applications from both safety and business dimensions is a top obstacle for the enterprise today. This is especially challenging for companies when leveraging advanced techniques like fine-tuning and RAG, maintaining LLM versions, and coordinating across teams including machine learning, governance, and business domain experts.
“The technology is not production-ready for business scenarios. Enterprise leaders are rightfully afraid of making headlines because their generative AI applications go rogue. Reputational harm keeps them up at night,” said Ariadna Font Llitjós, co-founder & CEO of Alinia. “Using a process known as Alignment, we ensure AI systems adhere to expected behaviors, policies, and regulatory requirements – helping enterprises seize the promise of this new technology while minimizing the risks and potential harm.”
Founded in 2023, Alinia Alignment Platform aims to provide an integral AI governance approach – which currently requires multiple tools – ranging from evaluation and real-time monitoring to cutting-edge optimization techniques, coupled with documentation features facilitating compliance at every stage of the LLM lifecycle. Enabling customers to have clear and accurate evidence of how LLM-powered applications behave in different enterprise scenarios, for specific tasks, and abiding by specific rules, is the first step towards alignment for the enterprise. 
“In my experience at Hugging Face, governance and safety aspects of LLM development require an incredible amount of time and effort. But when you are about to release a foundation model that will be used millions of times, governance and safety are non-negotiables,” addedCarlos Muñoz Ferrandis, co-founder & COO.
With an initial focus on enabling subject-matter experts to guide and validate the performance and safety of their generative AI applications, Alinia will use the funding to hire additional talent to build the Alinia Alignment Platform to create an end-to-end alignment process focused on safety and regulatory requirements –  enabling safe and inclusive use of generative AI across LLM modalities and languages. 
Fred Hagenauer, Partner with Speedinvest, said: “Ari and Carlos have some of the best profiles we´ve seen to tackle the alignment problem in the generative AI space. Their experience and knowledge of the early days of enterprise AI, RAI, and the  LLM governance required today is something truly unique. For us, it was a no-brainer to be the first ones to support them.”
Charles Hudson, Managing Partner with Precursor Ventures, commented: “Alinia brings the confidence that enterprise lacks today to deploy generative AI in a safe and trustworthy way. Being able to control and steer these powerful agents is such a challenge, but they have the experience needed and are willing to take it head-on.”
Stefano De Marzo

Anyformat Secures €3.3M to Scale Document Intelligence

By Ekaterina Pisareva
November 27, 2025
Anyformat team
Credits: Anyformat official website
Spanish AI startup Anyformat has closed a €3.3 million seed round to accelerate the development of its document intelligence platform. The funding was led by Kibo Ventures, with continued support from 4Founders, Abac Nest Ventures, and Decelera Ventures, all of which participated in the company's previous round.
The Madrid-based company has built a generative AI platform that extracts and structures complex data from virtually any document type. Its technology enables large organizations to process contracts, invoices, emails, and presentations with what the company describes as human-level accuracy at machine speed, while keeping sensitive data under full organizational control.
A New Approach to Document Intelligence
What sets Anyformat apart is its ability to combine extracted document information with a company's existing proprietary data in real time. This means businesses can automate the analysis of unstructured documents while simultaneously cross-referencing them against internal records like customer databases, policies, product catalogs, or historical information.
For organizations juggling complex documentation across fragmented systems, this capability eliminates manual validation steps, reduces errors, and speeds up business-critical processes. The opportunity is significant: according to Dell Technologies' Innovation Catalyst report, 68% of Spanish companies are unable to turn their data into valuable, real-time insights.
"The document intelligence market is entering a new phase driven by generative AI and agentic systems. Anyformat has developed a unique technology that enables companies to automate critical processes while preserving data sovereignty, an essential requirement in Europe. The founders' vision and talent, combined with the vast potential of AI in a large and growing market, were key factors in our decision to invest," says Javier Torremocha, Co-founder and Managing Partner at Kibo Ventures.
Expanding Capabilities and European Reach
The fresh capital will fuel product development and grow the company's engineering and product teams. Priorities include strengthening advanced extraction capabilities and building proprietary error-detection models. Anyformat also plans to establish a dedicated sales team focused on Spain and the broader European Union market.
The company will continue investing in its privacy-first infrastructure and is working toward ISO 27001 certification, a requirement for many large corporations and public institutions.
Anyformat forecasts surpassing €1 million in annual recurring revenue in 2026, driven by European expansion and growing demand for automation, compliance tools, and advanced document processing solutions.
From Startup to Enterprise Partner
Founded by CEO Juan Huguet and CTO Diego Pérez, Anyformat has already secured projects with the Singapore Government, L'Oréal Group, and IAG Group. These partnerships demonstrate the platform's ability to operate in complex, high-demand environments.
"Documents are the backbone of any organization. Our goal is to help companies analyze them with human intelligence, machine speed, and the level of security required by modern regulations," says Huguet. Pérez adds: "We are building technology that goes far beyond simple document digitization."
Since raising €520,000 in June 2025, the company has significantly expanded its capabilities beyond traditional document extraction. It now positions itself as the only provider capable of real-time integration between extracted document data and enterprise systems.
Investors Back Long-Term Vision
The seed round brings together a strong syndicate of Spanish and European investors. Kibo Ventures led the round, while 4Founders, Abac Nest Ventures, and Decelera Ventures all doubled down on their earlier investments. The continued backing from all previous investors signals confidence in Anyformat's trajectory and market positioning.
The company continues refining its technology to convert any file into structured data for analytics, business intelligence, and operational workflows, always maintaining strict privacy and security standards.

​ What is anyformat?
anyformat is a document processing platform that lets you:
 Upload documents (PDFs, images, scans)
 Extract structured data using AI
 Validate and refine results
 Output clean data you can actually use
You don’t need to be technical. You don’t need to write code. You do need documents and a problem to solve. 
​ What can I do with anyformat?
With anyformat, you can:
 Extract data from invoices, receipts, IDs, contracts, and more
 Handle unstructured or messy documents
 Standardize data across different formats
 Build repeatable workflows for document processing
 Validate and improve quality over time
 Export structured data to your systems
All without managing OCR engines, brittle rules, or manual review processes. 
​ How anyformat works
At a simple level, anyformat works like this:
1
Upload a file
anyformat reads the document and understands its structure.
2
Define what you want
You describe the data you need using fields and instructions.
3
Refine & validate
Review results, fix mistakes, and improve accuracy.
4
Run at scale
Apply the same logic to thousands of documents.
5
Use the output
Export clean, structured data in formats your tools understand.
You can keep things simple, or go deeper as your needs grow. 
​ Key concepts you’ll learn here
Before diving in, it helps to understand a few core ideas:
 Files - The documents you upload
 Schemas - The structure of the data you want
 Fields - Individual pieces of information (date, amount, name…)
 Workflows - Repeatable processes to extract and validate data
 Outputs - The structured results you use elsewhere
Don’t worry, we’ll explain each one step by step. 
​ How to use this documentation
This documentation is designed as a learning path:
 New to anyformat? Start with Core Concepts.
 Already using the product? Jump directly to Workflows.
 Looking for power features? Check out the advanced sections.
You don’t need to read everything, just what helps you move forward.

Learn about structured results and export formats

Established in 2024 and based in Madrid, Spain, Anyformat focuses on document processing technologies. Alejandro Fernandez Rodriguez (COO), Juan Huguet Garcia (CEO), and Diego Perez Sastre (CTO) are the co-founders of the company. In November 2025, Anyformat raised EUR 3. 3 million in seed funding led by new investor Kibo Ventures, with participation from another new investor Decelera Ventures.
Returning investors 4Founders Capital and Abac Capital also participated in the round.
Anyformat specializes in advanced data extraction and structuring solutions, enabling organizations to derive insights from various document formats, including emails, PDFs, scans, reports, and audio recordings. The company’s core offering utilizes proprietary in-house models optimized for specific use cases, allowing for automated extraction of complex and heterogeneous documents without the risk of hallucination. This technology is particularly beneficial for industries requiring the processing of lengthy documents, such as financial services, healthcare, and insurance.
Anyformat serves a diverse client base, including leading companies across multiple sectors, who leverage the service to streamline processes such as data reconciliation, regulatory compliance, and customer relationship management. The products are deployed in private cloud or on-premises environments, ensuring data security and privacy while being utilized in global markets.
The revenue model of Anyformat is centered around B2B transactions, where enterprises engage the company’s services through subscription-based agreements or tailored partnerships.
The company plans to utilize the November 2025 funding to grow its engineering and product teams, which will help advance its roadmap for enhanced extraction features and the creation of unique error detection models.

Anyformat closes a €3.3 million seed round led by Kibo Ventures
anyformat_team
01/12/2025  |  sradmin
Anyformat,  a generative AI platform specialized in extracting and structuring complex data from any document, has closed a €3.3 million seed round led by Kibo Ventures, with follow-on participation from 4Founders, Abac Nest Ventures, and Decelera Ventures, all of which also supported the company’s previous round.
The company is building the next generation of document infrastructure powered by generative AI. Its platform enables operational teams in large organizations to process complex documents with human-level accuracy at machine speed, while retaining full control over sensitive data. The technology is designed for seamless deployment from day one, requiring minimal technical dependencies.
Since its previous €520,000 pre-seed round, Anyformat has expanded its capabilities well beyond traditional extraction. It is now the only provider that can combine extracted document information with internal, proprietary company data in real time. This means that contracts, invoices, or emails can be automatically analyzed while being cross-referenced with corporate databases such as customer records, policies, product catalogs, or historical data.
For organizations with complex documentation and fragmented systems, this capability eliminates manual validations, reduces errors, and accelerates critical business processes. This represents a real problem: according to Dell Technologies’ Innovation Catalyst report, 68% of Spanish companies fail to convert their data into valuable information in real time, highlighting the urgency for solutions such as Anyformat.
“”The document intelligence market is entering a new phase driven by generative AI and agentic systems. Anyformat has developed a unique technology that allows companies to automate critical processes while maintaining data sovereignty, a key issue in Europe. The vision and talent of the founders, whom we know from their time at Clarity.ai, a company also invested in by Kibo Ventures, combined with the possibilities offered by AI in a huge market, were key factors in our decision to invest,” says Javier Torremocha, Co-founder and Managing Partner of Kibo Ventures.,” says Javier Torremocha, partner at Kibo Ventures.
With a team of nine full-time employees, the startup continues to refine its technology to convert any document, presentation, or file into data ready for analysis, business intelligence, or internal operations, always with the strictest privacy controls.
Growth underway: €1 million ARR forecast for 2026
Anyformat aims to exceed €1 million in Annual Recurring Revenue (ARR) in 2026, supported by its commercial expansion in Europe and the increase in use cases linked to automation, compliance, and advanced document processing.
Juan Huguet, CEO and co-founder of Anyformat, says “Documents are the backbone of any organization. Our goal is to enable companies to analyze them with human intelligence, machine speed, and the security required by regulations. We are building the new European document infrastructure.” Diego Pérez, co-founder and CTO of the company, adds, “We are developing technology capable of going far beyond document digitization.”
The investment: technology, expansion, and compliance
This new funding will enable the company to accelerate its product development and strengthen areas critical to its growth. A significant portion of the funds will be used to expand the engineering and product team, driving its roadmap in advanced extraction capabilities and the development of proprietary error detection models. At the same time, Anyformat will build a highly focused sales team in Spain and the European Union to capitalize on the growing demand for intelligent data processing solutions.
In addition, the company will continue to invest in its privacy-first infrastructure, strengthening security processes and advancing certifications such as ISO 27001, which is key to working with large corporations and public administrations.
With this round of funding, Anyformat is accelerating its path to becoming the European benchmark in document AI. The company is positioning its Agentic OCR as the new standard for automating critical processes in organizations that cannot compromise on accuracy or data control. In a market moving towards more autonomous, private, and multimodal solutions, Anyformat is setting the course for how generative AI will be applied in document management over the next decade.

supporting_evidence.raw_content:
Logo
Logo

Logo
Madrid-based Anyformat raises €520k to simplify the automation of unstructured data extraction
Stefano De Marzo

Anyformat, a Spanish startup betting on a new era of collaboration between people and artificial intelligence, has announced the raising of €520k in a pre-seed financing round. This round, led by 4Founders Capital, Abac Nest Ventures, and prominent business angels, will allow anyformat to accelerate the development of its generative artificial intelligence platform, designed to transform the way companies manage and analyze unstructured data.
With this injection of capital, anyformat is consolidating itself as a key solution to automate the extraction and analysis of complex data, enabling any user – without the need for technical knowledge – to convert large volumes of unstructured information into strategic insights. This marks a crucial step for organizations looking to optimize processes and improve data-driven decision making.
According to the Data Cube report “Spain Artificial Intelligence Market Growth and Performance”, the artificial intelligence market in Spain is expected to reach $26.56 billion by 2032, with a compound annual growth rate of 27.4%. This growth environment, combined with the exponential increase in the generation of unstructured data, reinforces the strategic relevance of anyformat for companies seeking operational agility and efficiency.
Juan Huguet, CEO of anyformat, said: “Our mission is clear: to enable organizations to adopt hybrid intelligence by allowing AI to do the heavy lifting of extracting and structuring data, and getting people to focus on solving strategic and creative problems. With anyformat, companies access valuable information quickly and without complications.”
The anyformat platform automates the extraction of information contained in documents, presentations or even voice recordings, simplifying what previously required a lot of manual work or advanced technical knowledge. In the words of Alejandro Fernández, COO of anyformat, “Many companies are held back by the difficulty of managing unstructured data. With our solution, we make that information immediately available to teams, ready to be integrated into tools such as Excel or business intelligence platforms.”
In addition to its focus on usability, anyformat is designed to scale easily, ensuring simple integration with existing systems. Diego Pérez, CTO of anyformat, added: “Our priority is to optimize workflows without compromising data security or privacy. We know that trust is key, and we ensure that information is handled responsibly and efficiently, allowing organizations to grow sustainably.”
Investors recognize anyformat’s disruptive potential. Joan Pina, partner at Abac Nest Ventures, commented: “At Abac Nest Ventures we have bet on anyformat because we believe in the vision of its founders and in the impact that its technology can have on the industry. Turning unstructured data into actionable knowledge is a growing need, and companies using anyformat are gaining competitiveness by quickly and efficiently integrating AI into their processes.”
With the new funding, anyformat is preparing to expand its team and operations, with an eye toward consolidating its presence in Spain and exploring opportunities in international markets. “At anyformat, we are committed to transforming unstructured information into a competitive advantage, allowing companies to not only access valuable information, but to use it intelligently to make strategic decisions that drive their growth and success,” concluded Juan Huguet.
Stefano De Marzo

supporting_evidence.raw_content:
Capital-Riesgo.es

Anyformat closes a €3.3M seed round led by Kibo Ventures

Anyformat, the generative AI platform specialized in extracting and structuring complex data from any document, has closed a €3.3 million seed round led by Kibo Ventures, with follow-on participation from 4Founders, Abac Nest Ventures and Decelera Ventures, funds that also invested in the previous round.
The company is leading a new generation of document infrastructure based on generative AI, enabling the operational teams of large corporations to work with complex documents with human-level precision and machine-level speed, while always maintaining full control over their data. This is achieved thanks to technology designed to be deployed from day one, with no friction or technical dependencies.
Since its previous round, where it raised €520,000 in pre-seed funding, Anyformat has significantly expanded its value proposition beyond traditional data extraction. Today, the company is the only provider that enables, in real time, the combination of the information extracted from a document with a company’s internal and proprietary data. This means that a contract, invoice, or email can be automatically analyzed while being cross-referenced directly with corporate databases such as customer records, policies, catalogues or historical data.
For organizations with complex documentation and fragmented systems, this capability eliminates manual validations, reduces errors, and accelerates critical business processes. This addresses a real and widespread problem: according to Dell Technologies’ Innovation Catalyst report, 68% of Spanish companies are unable to turn their data into valuable real-time information, highlighting the urgency of solutions like Anyformat.
“The document intelligence market is entering a new phase driven by generative AI and agentic systems. Anyformat has developed a differentiated technology that enables companies to automate critical processes while maintaining data sovereignty, a key aspect in Europe. The vision and talent of the founders, whom we know from their time at Clarity.ai —another company backed by Kibo Ventures— together with the enormous opportunities offered by AI in a large market, were decisive in our decision to invest,” said Javier Torremocha, Co-founder and Managing Partner at Kibo Ventures.
With a team of nine full-time employees, the startup continues refining its technology to convert any document, presentation, or file into data ready for analysis, business intelligence or internal operations, while complying with the strictest privacy controls.
Growth underway: forecast of €1M ARR by 2026
Anyformat aims to surpass €1 million in Annual Recurring Revenue (ARR) in 2026, supported by its commercial expansion across Europe and the growth of use cases related to automation, compliance and advanced document processing.
“Documents are the backbone of every organization. Our goal is to enable companies to analyze them with the intelligence of a human, the speed of a machine and the level of security required by regulation. We are building the new European document infrastructure,” said Juan Huguet, CEO and co-founder of Anyformat.  
For his part, Diego Pérez, co-founder and CTO, added: “We are developing technology capable of going far beyond simple document digitization.”
The investment: technology, expansion and compliance
This new funding will enable the company to accelerate product development and strengthen the areas most critical to its growth. A significant portion of the capital will go toward expanding the engineering and product teams, boosting its roadmap in advanced extraction capabilities and in developing proprietary error-detection models. At the same time, Anyformat will build a highly focused commercial team in Spain and the European Union to capitalize on the growing demand for intelligent data processing solutions.
In addition, the company will continue investing in its privacy-first infrastructure, reinforcing security processes and advancing certifications such as ISO 27001, which are essential for working with large corporations and public administrations.
With this round, Anyformat accelerates its path to becoming the leading European reference in document AI. The company is positioning its Agentic OCR as the new standard for automating critical processes in organizations that cannot compromise on precision or data control. In a market moving toward more autonomous, private and multimodal solutions, Anyformat is setting the direction for how generative AI will be applied to document management over the next decade.
About Anyformat
Anyformat is a Spanish generative AI startup based in Madrid, founded in 2024 by Juan Huguet and Diego Pérez. It offers a platform that automates the extraction and structuring of information contained in unstructured data sources, enabling its analysis without technical expertise. Its technology transforms these data into formats ready for integration into business intelligence tools and spreadsheets, reducing manual processing from hours to minutes. Its mission is to unlock the value of unstructured data, helping companies make strategic decisions and optimize their workflows.  
For more information, visit 
Fuente: Kibo Ventures

55 Best Ai Startups in Madrid to Watch in 2026
The Definitive Seedtable Ranking of Ai Startups in Madrid
We track 71,000+ companies and rank them dynamically using our Seedtable Score – a score that uses quantitative and qualitative data points to signal the momentum behind a company. We then monitor the list manually leveraging our expertise as founders and investors.
There are 55 start-ups with an aggregate funding of $2.8b. The average funding per company in this subset is $51.6m.
The list encompasses 404 founders. 1863 executives. It includes data on 2267 professionals within these filtered companies.
Last update to the database: Mar 10, 2026. See changelog.

​ What is anyformat?
anyformat is a document processing platform that lets you:
 Upload documents (PDFs, images, scans)
 Extract structured data using AI
 Validate and refine results
 Output clean data you can actually use
You don’t need to be technical. You don’t need to write code. You do need documents and a problem to solve. 
​ What can I do with anyformat?
With anyformat, you can:
 Extract data from invoices, receipts, IDs, contracts, and more
 Handle unstructured or messy documents
 Standardize data across different formats
 Build repeatable workflows for document processing
 Validate and improve quality over time
 Export structured data to your systems
All without managing OCR engines, brittle rules, or manual review processes. 
​ How anyformat works
At a simple level, anyformat works like this:
1
Upload a file
anyformat reads the document and understands its structure.
2
Define what you want
You describe the data you need using fields and instructions.
3
Refine & validate
Review results, fix mistakes, and improve accuracy.
4
Run at scale
Apply the same logic to thousands of documents.
5
Use the output
Export clean, structured data in formats your tools understand.
You can keep things simple, or go deeper as your needs grow. 
​ Key concepts you’ll learn here
Before diving in, it helps to understand a few core ideas:
 Files - The documents you upload
 Schemas - The structure of the data you want
 Fields - Individual pieces of information (date, amount, name…)
 Workflows - Repeatable processes to extract and validate data
 Outputs - The structured results you use elsewhere
Don’t worry, we’ll explain each one step by step. 
​ How to use this documentation
This documentation is designed as a learning path:
 New to anyformat? Start with Core Concepts.
 Already using the product? Jump directly to Workflows.
 Looking for power features? Check out the advanced sections.
You don’t need to read everything, just what helps you move forward.

Anyformat - overview
Established
2024
Location
Madrid, -, Spain
Primary Industry
Software
About
Established in 2024 and based in Madrid, Spain, Anyformat focuses on document processing technologies. Alejandro Fernandez Rodriguez (COO), Juan Huguet Garcia (CEO), and Diego Perez Sastre (CTO) are the co-founders of the company. In November 2025, Anyformat raised EUR 3. 3 million in seed funding led by new investor Kibo Ventures, with participation from another new investor Decelera Ventures.
Returning investors 4Founders Capital and Abac Capital also participated in the round.
Anyformat specializes in advanced data extraction and structuring solutions, enabling organizations to derive insights from various document formats, including emails, PDFs, scans, reports, and audio recordings. The company’s core offering utilizes proprietary in-house models optimized for specific use cases, allowing for automated extraction of complex and heterogeneous documents without the risk of hallucination. This technology is particularly beneficial for industries requiring the processing of lengthy documents, such as financial services, healthcare, and insurance.
Anyformat serves a diverse client base, including leading companies across multiple sectors, who leverage the service to streamline processes such as data reconciliation, regulatory compliance, and customer relationship management. The products are deployed in private cloud or on-premises environments, ensuring data security and privacy while being utilized in global markets.
The revenue model of Anyformat is centered around B2B transactions, where enterprises engage the company’s services through subscription-based agreements or tailored partnerships.
The company plans to utilize the November 2025 funding to grow its engineering and product teams, which will help advance its roadmap for enhanced extraction features and the creation of unique error detection models.

Home » Funding news
Anyformat Secures €3.3M to Scale Document Intelligence

By Ekaterina Pisareva
November 27, 2025
Anyformat team
Credits: Anyformat official website
Spanish AI startup Anyformat has closed a €3.3 million seed round to accelerate the development of its document intelligence platform. The funding was led by Kibo Ventures, with continued support from 4Founders, Abac Nest Ventures, and Decelera Ventures, all of which participated in the company's previous round.
The Madrid-based company has built a generative AI platform that extracts and structures complex data from virtually any document type. Its technology enables large organizations to process contracts, invoices, emails, and presentations with what the company describes as human-level accuracy at machine speed, while keeping sensitive data under full organizational control.
A New Approach to Document Intelligence
What sets Anyformat apart is its ability to combine extracted document information with a company's existing proprietary data in real time. This means businesses can automate the analysis of unstructured documents while simultaneously cross-referencing them against internal records like customer databases, policies, product catalogs, or historical information.
For organizations juggling complex documentation across fragmented systems, this capability eliminates manual validation steps, reduces errors, and speeds up business-critical processes. The opportunity is significant: according to Dell Technologies' Innovation Catalyst report, 68% of Spanish companies are unable to turn their data into valuable, real-time insights.
"The document intelligence market is entering a new phase driven by generative AI and agentic systems. Anyformat has developed a unique technology that enables companies to automate critical processes while preserving data sovereignty, an essential requirement in Europe. The founders' vision and talent, combined with the vast potential of AI in a large and growing market, were key factors in our decision to invest," says Javier Torremocha, Co-founder and Managing Partner at Kibo Ventures.
Expanding Capabilities and European Reach
The fresh capital will fuel product development and grow the company's engineering and product teams. Priorities include strengthening advanced extraction capabilities and building proprietary error-detection models. Anyformat also plans to establish a dedicated sales team focused on Spain and the broader European Union market.
The company will continue investing in its privacy-first infrastructure and is working toward ISO 27001 certification, a requirement for many large corporations and public institutions.
Anyformat forecasts surpassing €1 million in annual recurring revenue in 2026, driven by European expansion and growing demand for automation, compliance tools, and advanced document processing solutions.
From Startup to Enterprise Partner
Founded by CEO Juan Huguet and CTO Diego Pérez, Anyformat has already secured projects with the Singapore Government, L'Oréal Group, and IAG Group. These partnerships demonstrate the platform's ability to operate in complex, high-demand environments.
"Documents are the backbone of any organization. Our goal is to help companies analyze them with human intelligence, machine speed, and the level of security required by modern regulations," says Huguet. Pérez adds: "We are building technology that goes far beyond simple document digitization."
Since raising €520,000 in June 2025, the company has significantly expanded its capabilities beyond traditional document extraction. It now positions itself as the only provider capable of real-time integration between extracted document data and enterprise systems.
Investors Back Long-Term Vision
The seed round brings together a strong syndicate of Spanish and European investors. Kibo Ventures led the round, while 4Founders, Abac Nest Ventures, and Decelera Ventures all doubled down on their earlier investments. The continued backing from all previous investors signals confidence in Anyformat's trajectory and market positioning.
The company continues refining its technology to convert any file into structured data for analytics, business intelligence, and operational workflows, always maintaining strict privacy and security standards.

Anyformat - overview
Established
2024
Location
Madrid, -, Spain
Primary Industry
Software
About
Established in 2024 and based in Madrid, Spain, Anyformat focuses on document processing technologies. Alejandro Fernandez Rodriguez (COO), Juan Huguet Garcia (CEO), and Diego Perez Sastre (CTO) are the co-founders of the company. In November 2025, Anyformat raised EUR 3. 3 million in seed funding led by new investor Kibo Ventures, with participation from another new investor Decelera Ventures.
Returning investors 4Founders Capital and Abac Capital also participated in the round.
Anyformat specializes in advanced data extraction and structuring solutions, enabling organizations to derive insights from various document formats, including emails, PDFs, scans, reports, and audio recordings. The company’s core offering utilizes proprietary in-house models optimized for specific use cases, allowing for automated extraction of complex and heterogeneous documents without the risk of hallucination. This technology is particularly beneficial for industries requiring the processing of lengthy documents, such as financial services, healthcare, and insurance.
Anyformat serves a diverse client base, including leading companies across multiple sectors, who leverage the service to streamline processes such as data reconciliation, regulatory compliance, and customer relationship management. The products are deployed in private cloud or on-premises environments, ensuring data security and privacy while being utilized in global markets.
The revenue model of Anyformat is centered around B2B transactions, where enterprises engage the company’s services through subscription-based agreements or tailored partnerships.
The company plans to utilize the November 2025 funding to grow its engineering and product teams, which will help advance its roadmap for enhanced extraction features and the creation of unique error detection models.

55 Best Ai Startups in Madrid to Watch in 2026
The Definitive Seedtable Ranking of Ai Startups in Madrid
We track 71,000+ companies and rank them dynamically using our Seedtable Score – a score that uses quantitative and qualitative data points to signal the momentum behind a company. We then monitor the list manually leveraging our expertise as founders and investors.
There are 55 start-ups with an aggregate funding of $2.8b. The average funding per company in this subset is $51.6m.
The list encompasses 404 founders. 1863 executives. It includes data on 2267 professionals within these filtered companies.

HomeFundingWith €4 million in funding, Kabilio scales AI tools helping Spanish accounting...
FundingSpain-Startups
With €4 million in funding, Kabilio scales AI tools helping Spanish accounting firms lift productivity by up to 50%
By David Cendon Garcia
Share
FacebookTwitterWhatsAppLinkedin
EU-Startups has learned that Kabilio, a Barcelona-based company specialising in the application of AI to automate accounting and tax processes, has closed a €4 million pre-Seed funding round.
The round was led by international venture capital funds Visionaries Club and Picus Capital. The round also includes €200k in public funding from ENISA, reportedly making it one of the largest pre-Seed rounds in Spain.
“We have seen firsthand the challenge of attracting and retaining talent in accounting firms because the work is concentrated on repetitive tasks and peak workloads. We have also perceived a certain fear of adopting AI.
“It is precisely to change this that we created ​Kabilio, and the closing of this funding round gives us the definitive push to help these firms alleviate their teams’ workload and allow them to focus on providing more value to their clients, which is what they are truly trained for,” states Jose Ojeda, co-CEO and founder of Kabilio.
The funding round for Kabilio fits into a broader European movement of 2025 investments in AI-driven financial software.
Comparable activity includes Bluebook (Sweden), which raised €2.4 million pre-Seed to advance its self-driving accounting platform; Stacks (Netherlands), which secured €9.5 million to streamline month-end financial close processes using AI; and Plino (Italy), which obtained €650k to enhance SMEs’ financial planning and analysis.
Within this context, Kabilio’s €4 million pre-Seed is comparatively substantial, signalling investor confidence in AI solutions addressing accounting inefficiencies.
While other notable rounds have occurred in Northern and Central Europe, Kabilio’s success highlights Spain’s emerging role in this segment, marking one of the country’s largest early-stage financings in AI-powered accounting.
Robert Jäckle, Partner at Visionaries Club, highlights: “Kabilio brings AI to the heart of advisory firms not to replace accountants, but to make their work smarter and more valuable. They are not creating ‘hype’; they are solving a real operational problem. We believe Kabilio can become the leading platform for modern accounting in Europe.”
Founded in 2024 by Jose Ojeda (formerly of McKinsey, Rocket Internet, and 011h) and Álex  
Valls (formerly of Social Point, Exoticca, and 011h), the startup has developed a platform  
that uses generative AI to help accounting firms and their clients modernise the way  
they work together.
Kabilio was born from the conviction that AI will transform the advisory sector and from a commitment to support them in this transition. This mission has already earned the trust of nearly 100 accounting firms using its platform, who are seeing a productivity increase of up to 50%.
Florian Reichert, Managing Director at Picus Capital, adds that the platform “will enable advisors to collaborate more efficiently with their clients, leveraging AI to automate the most tedious tasks and generate strategic value”.
In Spain, there are approximately 65,000 accounting and tax advisory firms that work with millions of SMEs and freelancers. The current workflow presents significant inefficiencies: clients must manually collect and send information, which, due to their operational load, often happens late or incompletely.
This is exacerbated during quarterly peak periods, when advisors must concentrate a great deal of effort on checks and manual data entry within a few days.
Kabilio currently features three integrated products:
 Intelligent invoice processing: Allows receiving documents from multiple channels (email, Dropbox, etc.) and processing even the most complex invoices, such as intra-community and extra-community, reverse charge VAT, or multiple invoices in a single file. Furthermore, it suggests appropriate accounting accounts based on the content of each invoice and the company’s activity, with 97% accuracy.
 Advanced bank reconciliation: Securely connects with 99% of Spanish banks, reducing the need to request statements. The platform suggests matches between invoices and transactions using AI rules, allowing the advisor to view and edit all information in a single environment.
 Verifactu-Compliant invoicing: A modern, intuitive, and cost-effective product for SMEs and freelancers that allows for issuing customised invoices and synchronises​ all information with the advisor in real-time, eliminating the manual sending of data.
Additionally, the company is piloting its AI agent, Kabi, with several clients, which is being officially presented at Accountex Spain 2025. In its initial phase, it will allow users to query and extract information from the platform using natural language, and future versions will enable broader actions.
“We are experiencing a technological turning point similar to the arrival of software in companies two decades ago. Artificial intelligence will not only optimise processes but will also change the nature of work and the relationship between advisory firms and their clients. Our goal is to build the infrastructure that makes this new paradigm possible”, said Álex Valls, co-CEO and co-founder of Kabilio.

supporting_evidence.raw_content:
Logo
Logo
Logo

Logo
Barcelona-based Alinia raises €2.2 million pre-seed to enable safe deployment of genAI
Stefano De Marzo

Alinia AI, an alignment platform enabling companies’ safe and controlled deployment of generative AI, guided by their policies and business preferences, announces a successful €2.2 million pre-seed funding round. Speedinvest and Precursor led the round with participation from KFund and notable angels including Clem Délangue & Thom Wolf (Hugging Face Co-Founders), Tom Preston (GitHub Co-Founder), Xavier Amatriain (Google Core AI VP), and Oriol Vinyals (Google DeepMind VP of Research).
Significant adoption of generative AI is underway with spending set to hit $143 billion by 2027, but its rapid adoption comes with significant challenges. The problem? Managing control and safety in the face of risks to prevent inappropriate or brand-damaging content. Additionally, navigating upcoming regulatory efforts like the EU AI Act, Biden´s Executive Order, or Canada´s Bill C-27, which demand rigorous safety checks and clear documentation, also adds hurdles to mass enterprise adoption. 
Evaluating the performance and reliability of generative AI applications from both safety and business dimensions is a top obstacle for the enterprise today. This is especially challenging for companies when leveraging advanced techniques like fine-tuning and RAG, maintaining LLM versions, and coordinating across teams including machine learning, governance, and business domain experts.
“The technology is not production-ready for business scenarios. Enterprise leaders are rightfully afraid of making headlines because their generative AI applications go rogue. Reputational harm keeps them up at night,” said Ariadna Font Llitjós, co-founder & CEO of Alinia. “Using a process known as Alignment, we ensure AI systems adhere to expected behaviors, policies, and regulatory requirements – helping enterprises seize the promise of this new technology while minimizing the risks and potential harm.”
Founded in 2023, Alinia Alignment Platform aims to provide an integral AI governance approach – which currently requires multiple tools – ranging from evaluation and real-time monitoring to cutting-edge optimization techniques, coupled with documentation features facilitating compliance at every stage of the LLM lifecycle. Enabling customers to have clear and accurate evidence of how LLM-powered applications behave in different enterprise scenarios, for specific tasks, and abiding by specific rules, is the first step towards alignment for the enterprise. 
“In my experience at Hugging Face, governance and safety aspects of LLM development require an incredible amount of time and effort. But when you are about to release a foundation model that will be used millions of times, governance and safety are non-negotiables,” addedCarlos Muñoz Ferrandis, co-founder & COO.
With an initial focus on enabling subject-matter experts to guide and validate the performance and safety of their generative AI applications, Alinia will use the funding to hire additional talent to build the Alinia Alignment Platform to create an end-to-end alignment process focused on safety and regulatory requirements –  enabling safe and inclusive use of generative AI across LLM modalities and languages. 
Fred Hagenauer, Partner with Speedinvest, said: “Ari and Carlos have some of the best profiles we´ve seen to tackle the alignment problem in the generative AI space. Their experience and knowledge of the early days of enterprise AI, RAI, and the  LLM governance required today is something truly unique. For us, it was a no-brainer to be the first ones to support them.”
Charles Hudson, Managing Partner with Precursor Ventures, commented: “Alinia brings the confidence that enterprise lacks today to deploy generative AI in a safe and trustworthy way. Being able to control and steer these powerful agents is such a challenge, but they have the experience needed and are willing to take it head-on.”
Stefano De Marzo

Anyformat Secures €3.3M to Scale Document Intelligence

By Ekaterina Pisareva
November 27, 2025
Anyformat team
Credits: Anyformat official website
Spanish AI startup Anyformat has closed a €3.3 million seed round to accelerate the development of its document intelligence platform. The funding was led by Kibo Ventures, with continued support from 4Founders, Abac Nest Ventures, and Decelera Ventures, all of which participated in the company's previous round.
The Madrid-based company has built a generative AI platform that extracts and structures complex data from virtually any document type. Its technology enables large organizations to process contracts, invoices, emails, and presentations with what the company describes as human-level accuracy at machine speed, while keeping sensitive data under full organizational control.
A New Approach to Document Intelligence
What sets Anyformat apart is its ability to combine extracted document information with a company's existing proprietary data in real time. This means businesses can automate the analysis of unstructured documents while simultaneously cross-referencing them against internal records like customer databases, policies, product catalogs, or historical information.
For organizations juggling complex documentation across fragmented systems, this capability eliminates manual validation steps, reduces errors, and speeds up business-critical processes. The opportunity is significant: according to Dell Technologies' Innovation Catalyst report, 68% of Spanish companies are unable to turn their data into valuable, real-time insights.
"The document intelligence market is entering a new phase driven by generative AI and agentic systems. Anyformat has developed a unique technology that enables companies to automate critical processes while preserving data sovereignty, an essential requirement in Europe. The founders' vision and talent, combined with the vast potential of AI in a large and growing market, were key factors in our decision to invest," says Javier Torremocha, Co-founder and Managing Partner at Kibo Ventures.
Expanding Capabilities and European Reach
The fresh capital will fuel product development and grow the company's engineering and product teams. Priorities include strengthening advanced extraction capabilities and building proprietary error-detection models. Anyformat also plans to establish a dedicated sales team focused on Spain and the broader European Union market.
The company will continue investing in its privacy-first infrastructure and is working toward ISO 27001 certification, a requirement for many large corporations and public institutions.
Anyformat forecasts surpassing €1 million in annual recurring revenue in 2026, driven by European expansion and growing demand for automation, compliance tools, and advanced document processing solutions.
From Startup to Enterprise Partner
Founded by CEO Juan Huguet and CTO Diego Pérez, Anyformat has already secured projects with the Singapore Government, L'Oréal Group, and IAG Group. These partnerships demonstrate the platform's ability to operate in complex, high-demand environments.
"Documents are the backbone of any organization. Our goal is to help companies analyze them with human intelligence, machine speed, and the level of security required by modern regulations," says Huguet. Pérez adds: "We are building technology that goes far beyond simple document digitization."
Since raising €520,000 in June 2025, the company has significantly expanded its capabilities beyond traditional document extraction. It now positions itself as the only provider capable of real-time integration between extracted document data and enterprise systems.
Investors Back Long-Term Vision
The seed round brings together a strong syndicate of Spanish and European investors. Kibo Ventures led the round, while 4Founders, Abac Nest Ventures, and Decelera Ventures all doubled down on their earlier investments. The continued backing from all previous investors signals confidence in Anyformat's trajectory and market positioning.
The company continues refining its technology to convert any file into structured data for analytics, business intelligence, and operational workflows, always maintaining strict privacy and security standards.
More about:  AI | Europe | Funding | Generative AI | Seed Funding | Spain | Startups | Venture capital

​ What is anyformat?
anyformat is a document processing platform that lets you:
 Upload documents (PDFs, images, scans)
 Extract structured data using AI
 Validate and refine results
 Output clean data you can actually use
You don’t need to be technical. You don’t need to write code. You do need documents and a problem to solve. 
​ What can I do with anyformat?
With anyformat, you can:
 Extract data from invoices, receipts, IDs, contracts, and more
 Handle unstructured or messy documents
 Standardize data across different formats
 Build repeatable workflows for document processing
 Validate and improve quality over time
 Export structured data to your systems
All without managing OCR engines, brittle rules, or manual review processes. 
​ How anyformat works
At a simple level, anyformat works like this:
1
Upload a file
anyformat reads the document and understands its structure.
2
Define what you want
You describe the data you need using fields and instructions.
3
Refine & validate
Review results, fix mistakes, and improve accuracy.
4
Run at scale
Apply the same logic to thousands of documents.
5
Use the output
Export clean, structured data in formats your tools understand.
You can keep things simple, or go deeper as your needs grow. 
​ Key concepts you’ll learn here
Before diving in, it helps to understand a few core ideas:
 Files - The documents you upload
 Schemas - The structure of the data you want
 Fields - Individual pieces of information (date, amount, name…)
 Workflows - Repeatable processes to extract and validate data
 Outputs - The structured results you use elsewhere
Don’t worry, we’ll explain each one step by step. 
​ How to use this documentation
This documentation is designed as a learning path:
 New to anyformat? Start with Core Concepts.
 Already using the product? Jump directly to Workflows.
 Looking for power features? Check out the advanced sections.
You don’t need to read everything, just what helps you move forward.

Learn about structured results and export formats
Data and Structure
Define schemas and fields for your workflows

Anyformat - overview
Established
2024
Location
Madrid, -, Spain
Primary Industry
Software
About
Established in 2024 and based in Madrid, Spain, Anyformat focuses on document processing technologies. Alejandro Fernandez Rodriguez (COO), Juan Huguet Garcia (CEO), and Diego Perez Sastre (CTO) are the co-founders of the company. In November 2025, Anyformat raised EUR 3. 3 million in seed funding led by new investor Kibo Ventures, with participation from another new investor Decelera Ventures.
Returning investors 4Founders Capital and Abac Capital also participated in the round.
Anyformat specializes in advanced data extraction and structuring solutions, enabling organizations to derive insights from various document formats, including emails, PDFs, scans, reports, and audio recordings. The company’s core offering utilizes proprietary in-house models optimized for specific use cases, allowing for automated extraction of complex and heterogeneous documents without the risk of hallucination. This technology is particularly beneficial for industries requiring the processing of lengthy documents, such as financial services, healthcare, and insurance.
Anyformat serves a diverse client base, including leading companies across multiple sectors, who leverage the service to streamline processes such as data reconciliation, regulatory compliance, and customer relationship management. The products are deployed in private cloud or on-premises environments, ensuring data security and privacy while being utilized in global markets.
The revenue model of Anyformat is centered around B2B transactions, where enterprises engage the company’s services through subscription-based agreements or tailored partnerships.
The company plans to utilize the November 2025 funding to grow its engineering and product teams, which will help advance its roadmap for enhanced extraction features and the creation of unique error detection models.

Anyformat closes a €3.3 million seed round led by Kibo Ventures
anyformat_team
01/12/2025  |  sradmin
Anyformat,  a generative AI platform specialized in extracting and structuring complex data from any document, has closed a €3.3 million seed round led by Kibo Ventures, with follow-on participation from 4Founders, Abac Nest Ventures, and Decelera Ventures, all of which also supported the company’s previous round.
The company is building the next generation of document infrastructure powered by generative AI. Its platform enables operational teams in large organizations to process complex documents with human-level accuracy at machine speed, while retaining full control over sensitive data. The technology is designed for seamless deployment from day one, requiring minimal technical dependencies.
Since its previous €520,000 pre-seed round, Anyformat has expanded its capabilities well beyond traditional extraction. It is now the only provider that can combine extracted document information with internal, proprietary company data in real time. This means that contracts, invoices, or emails can be automatically analyzed while being cross-referenced with corporate databases such as customer records, policies, product catalogs, or historical data.
For organizations with complex documentation and fragmented systems, this capability eliminates manual validations, reduces errors, and accelerates critical business processes. This represents a real problem: according to Dell Technologies’ Innovation Catalyst report, 68% of Spanish companies fail to convert their data into valuable information in real time, highlighting the urgency for solutions such as Anyformat.
“”The document intelligence market is entering a new phase driven by generative AI and agentic systems. Anyformat has developed a unique technology that allows companies to automate critical processes while maintaining data sovereignty, a key issue in Europe. The vision and talent of the founders, whom we know from their time at Clarity.ai, a company also invested in by Kibo Ventures, combined with the possibilities offered by AI in a huge market, were key factors in our decision to invest,” says Javier Torremocha, Co-founder and Managing Partner of Kibo Ventures.,” says Javier Torremocha, partner at Kibo Ventures.
With a team of nine full-time employees, the startup continues to refine its technology to convert any document, presentation, or file into data ready for analysis, business intelligence, or internal operations, always with the strictest privacy controls.
Growth underway: €1 million ARR forecast for 2026
Anyformat aims to exceed €1 million in Annual Recurring Revenue (ARR) in 2026, supported by its commercial expansion in Europe and the increase in use cases linked to automation, compliance, and advanced document processing.
Juan Huguet, CEO and co-founder of Anyformat, says “Documents are the backbone of any organization. Our goal is to enable companies to analyze them with human intelligence, machine speed, and the security required by regulations. We are building the new European document infrastructure.” Diego Pérez, co-founder and CTO of the company, adds, “We are developing technology capable of going far beyond document digitization.”
The investment: technology, expansion, and compliance
This new funding will enable the company to accelerate its product development and strengthen areas critical to its growth. A significant portion of the funds will be used to expand the engineering and product team, driving its roadmap in advanced extraction capabilities and the development of proprietary error detection models. At the same time, Anyformat will build a highly focused sales team in Spain and the European Union to capitalize on the growing demand for intelligent data processing solutions.
In addition, the company will continue to invest in its privacy-first infrastructure, strengthening security processes and advancing certifications such as ISO 27001, which is key to working with large corporations and public administrations.
With this round of funding, Anyformat is accelerating its path to becoming the European benchmark in document AI. The company is positioning its Agentic OCR as the new standard for automating critical processes in organizations that cannot compromise on accuracy or data control. In a market moving towards more autonomous, private, and multimodal solutions, Anyformat is setting the course for how generative AI will be applied in document management over the next decade.

supporting_evidence.raw_content:
Logo
Logo
Logo

Logo
Madrid-based Anyformat raises €520k to simplify the automation of unstructured data extraction
Stefano De Marzo

Anyformat, a Spanish startup betting on a new era of collaboration between people and artificial intelligence, has announced the raising of €520k in a pre-seed financing round. This round, led by 4Founders Capital, Abac Nest Ventures, and prominent business angels, will allow anyformat to accelerate the development of its generative artificial intelligence platform, designed to transform the way companies manage and analyze unstructured data.
With this injection of capital, anyformat is consolidating itself as a key solution to automate the extraction and analysis of complex data, enabling any user – without the need for technical knowledge – to convert large volumes of unstructured information into strategic insights. This marks a crucial step for organizations looking to optimize processes and improve data-driven decision making.
According to the Data Cube report “Spain Artificial Intelligence Market Growth and Performance”, the artificial intelligence market in Spain is expected to reach $26.56 billion by 2032, with a compound annual growth rate of 27.4%. This growth environment, combined with the exponential increase in the generation of unstructured data, reinforces the strategic relevance of anyformat for companies seeking operational agility and efficiency.
Juan Huguet, CEO of anyformat, said: “Our mission is clear: to enable organizations to adopt hybrid intelligence by allowing AI to do the heavy lifting of extracting and structuring data, and getting people to focus on solving strategic and creative problems. With anyformat, companies access valuable information quickly and without complications.”
The anyformat platform automates the extraction of information contained in documents, presentations or even voice recordings, simplifying what previously required a lot of manual work or advanced technical knowledge. In the words of Alejandro Fernández, COO of anyformat, “Many companies are held back by the difficulty of managing unstructured data. With our solution, we make that information immediately available to teams, ready to be integrated into tools such as Excel or business intelligence platforms.”
In addition to its focus on usability, anyformat is designed to scale easily, ensuring simple integration with existing systems. Diego Pérez, CTO of anyformat, added: “Our priority is to optimize workflows without compromising data security or privacy. We know that trust is key, and we ensure that information is handled responsibly and efficiently, allowing organizations to grow sustainably.”
Investors recognize anyformat’s disruptive potential. Joan Pina, partner at Abac Nest Ventures, commented: “At Abac Nest Ventures we have bet on anyformat because we believe in the vision of its founders and in the impact that its technology can have on the industry. Turning unstructured data into actionable knowledge is a growing need, and companies using anyformat are gaining competitiveness by quickly and efficiently integrating AI into their processes.”
With the new funding, anyformat is preparing to expand its team and operations, with an eye toward consolidating its presence in Spain and exploring opportunities in international markets. “At anyformat, we are committed to transforming unstructured information into a competitive advantage, allowing companies to not only access valuable information, but to use it intelligently to make strategic decisions that drive their growth and success,” concluded Juan Huguet.
Stefano De Marzo

supporting_evidence.raw_content:
Capital-Riesgo.es

Anyformat closes a €3.3M seed round led by Kibo Ventures

Anyformat, the generative AI platform specialized in extracting and structuring complex data from any document, has closed a €3.3 million seed round led by Kibo Ventures, with follow-on participation from 4Founders, Abac Nest Ventures and Decelera Ventures, funds that also invested in the previous round.
The company is leading a new generation of document infrastructure based on generative AI, enabling the operational teams of large corporations to work with complex documents with human-level precision and machine-level speed, while always maintaining full control over their data. This is achieved thanks to technology designed to be deployed from day one, with no friction or technical dependencies.
Since its previous round, where it raised €520,000 in pre-seed funding, Anyformat has significantly expanded its value proposition beyond traditional data extraction. Today, the company is the only provider that enables, in real time, the combination of the information extracted from a document with a company’s internal and proprietary data. This means that a contract, invoice, or email can be automatically analyzed while being cross-referenced directly with corporate databases such as customer records, policies, catalogues or historical data.
For organizations with complex documentation and fragmented systems, this capability eliminates manual validations, reduces errors, and accelerates critical business processes. This addresses a real and widespread problem: according to Dell Technologies’ Innovation Catalyst report, 68% of Spanish companies are unable to turn their data into valuable real-time information, highlighting the urgency of solutions like Anyformat.
“The document intelligence market is entering a new phase driven by generative AI and agentic systems. Anyformat has developed a differentiated technology that enables companies to automate critical processes while maintaining data sovereignty, a key aspect in Europe. The vision and talent of the founders, whom we know from their time at Clarity.ai —another company backed by Kibo Ventures— together with the enormous opportunities offered by AI in a large market, were decisive in our decision to invest,” said Javier Torremocha, Co-founder and Managing Partner at Kibo Ventures.
With a team of nine full-time employees, the startup continues refining its technology to convert any document, presentation, or file into data ready for analysis, business intelligence or internal operations, while complying with the strictest privacy controls.
Growth underway: forecast of €1M ARR by 2026
Anyformat aims to surpass €1 million in Annual Recurring Revenue (ARR) in 2026, supported by its commercial expansion across Europe and the growth of use cases related to automation, compliance and advanced document processing.
“Documents are the backbone of every organization. Our goal is to enable companies to analyze them with the intelligence of a human, the speed of a machine and the level of security required by regulation. We are building the new European document infrastructure,” said Juan Huguet, CEO and co-founder of Anyformat.  
For his part, Diego Pérez, co-founder and CTO, added: “We are developing technology capable of going far beyond simple document digitization.”
The investment: technology, expansion and compliance
This new funding will enable the company to accelerate product development and strengthen the areas most critical to its growth. A significant portion of the capital will go toward expanding the engineering and product teams, boosting its roadmap in advanced extraction capabilities and in developing proprietary error-detection models. At the same time, Anyformat will build a highly focused commercial team in Spain and the European Union to capitalize on the growing demand for intelligent data processing solutions.
In addition, the company will continue investing in its privacy-first infrastructure, reinforcing security processes and advancing certifications such as ISO 27001, which are essential for working with large corporations and public administrations.
With this round, Anyformat accelerates its path to becoming the leading European reference in document AI. The company is positioning its Agentic OCR as the new standard for automating critical processes in organizations that cannot compromise on precision or data control. In a market moving toward more autonomous, private and multimodal solutions, Anyformat is setting the direction for how generative AI will be applied to document management over the next decade.
About Anyformat
Anyformat is a Spanish generative AI startup based in Madrid, founded in 2024 by Juan Huguet and Diego Pérez. It offers a platform that automates the extraction and structuring of information contained in unstructured data sources, enabling its analysis without technical expertise. Its technology transforms these data into formats ready for integration into business intelligence tools and spreadsheets, reducing manual processing from hours to minutes. Its mission is to unlock the value of unstructured data, helping companies make strategic decisions and optimize their workflows.  
For more information, visit 
Fuente: Kibo Ventures
